title: Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) Intrinsic Value — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Eli Lilly and Company (LLY).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Eli Lilly's Mounjaro/Zepbound franchise is creating the largest drug class in pharmaceutical history. GLP-1 agonists address obesity (a $100B+ TAM), diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and potentially A...

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC reflects Eli Lilly and Company's risk profile, including sector-specific volatility, competitive dynamics, and macroeconomic sensitivity.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes Eli Lilly and Company grows roughly in line with nominal GDP into perpetuity, reflecting the law of large numbers for a mature large-cap enterprise.

thesisParagraphs: Eli Lilly's Mounjaro/Zepbound franchise is creating the largest drug class in pharmaceutical history. GLP-1 agonists address obesity (a $100B+ TAM), diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and potentially Alzheimer's. Lilly has the best pipeline in pharma and is executing flawlessly on manufacturing scale-up.

However, the stock has more than tripled in two years, pricing in near-perfect execution. My aggressive 22% growth rate reflects genuine GLP-1 tailwinds, but the current price implies Lilly must sustain blockbuster growth for a decade while fending off competition from Novo Nordisk, Amgen, and eventual generics. The risk-reward at $820 is asymmetric to the downside.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is Lilly's growth rate so high at 22%?
faq.answer: GLP-1 drugs (Mounjaro, Zepbound) are growing 50%+ annually and represent a generational pharmaceutical opportunity. Even after normalization, this drug class should drive 20%+ revenue growth through 2030.

faq.question: Is Eli Lilly overvalued at $820?
faq.answer: By traditional DCF metrics, yes. The stock trades at 80x+ trailing FCF. Opus's intrinsic value is well below the current price, suggesting the market is pricing in a best-case scenario across all pipeline programs.

faq.question: What could go wrong?
faq.answer: Manufacturing constraints limiting supply, unexpected GLP-1 safety signals in long-term studies, or Medicare price negotiation under the IRA could all meaningfully impact the valuation thesis.
